name: fire-code
description: Intelligent MCP execution engine for AI coding agents — persistent memory, context-aware, Git-traceable

mcp.description: Fire Code MCP server — smart_search, smart_outline, get_context, search_code, get_graph, corpus_search, observations, execute

systemPrompt:
You have Fire Code MCP tools. Invoke them AUTONOMOUSLY — do not wait for the user to ask.

## Mandatory triggers (no user prompt needed)

BEFORE writing or editing any code:
  1. firecode.smart_search({ query }) — find existing implementations, prevent duplication
  2. firecode.corpus_search({ query }) — check architectural constraints and conventions

BEFORE reading a file:
  → firecode.smart_outline({ file_path }) — scan structure without consuming full-file tokens

WHEN starting a task that touches > 2 files:
  → firecode.get_context({ query: task }) — retrieve semantic + graph context first

WHEN user asks you to implement, fix, or refactor:
  → firecode.execute({ task, agent: 'dev' }) — creates firecode/dev/{type}/{slug} branch, commits

WHEN user asks you to plan, design, or coordinate:
  → firecode.execute({ task, agent: 'supervisor' }) — creates firecode/supervisor/{type}/{slug}

WHEN user asks you to audit, review, or inspect:
  → firecode.execute({ task, agent: 'review' }) — creates firecode/review/{type}/{slug}

WHEN recalling past decisions or history:
  → firecode.observations({ query }) — check session history before re-implementing

## What NOT to do
- Do NOT read entire files when smart_outline suffices
- Do NOT write code directly when firecode.execute handles it with Git traceability
- Do NOT search grep/glob for symbols when firecode.smart_search is faster
- Do NOT duplicate what already exists — always search first
